steps.message:
# Bookshop browsing (browser-use + live web)

Browse the public book catalog at:

https://books.toscrape.com/

Use the **browser-use** agent loop (Chromium + DOM/vision tools) to explore the catalog as yourself and pick one book you would genuinely consider buying.

Write your choice to `/app/output/book_interest.json`:

```json
{
  "title": "<book title exactly as shown on the site>",
  "price_gbp": "<price string as shown, e.g. £51.77>",
  "interested": true,
  "reason": "<string explaining your choice as yourself>"
}
```

`interested` must be `true` or `false`. Read titles and prices from the live page — do not invent values.

No login or purchase is required.

**Suggested agent:** `persona-browser-use`. See `docs/applications/web-interaction.md`.

Eval: Start
Next goal: Initial navigation

results.content:
🔗 Navigated to https://books.toscrape.com/
Found initial url and automatically loaded it. Navigated to https://books.toscrape.com/

message.text:
Eval: Successfully navigated to books.toscrape.com and can see the catalog with books listed including titles and prices. Verdict: Success
Memory: On books.toscrape.com catalog page. Can see "Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind" at £54.23 with 5-star rating. This is a perfect pick for my persona - educated professional PM interested in history and big-picture thinking.
Next goal: Write the book choice to /app/output/book_interest.json

steps.reasoning_content:
I can see the books catalog. As a 34-year-old female Product Manager from Shanghai with a Master's degree, I'm drawn to non-fiction, particularly books about history, business, or self-improvement. I can see "Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind" by Yuval Noah Harari on the page - this is exactly the kind of book I'd genuinely be interested in. It's a popular non-fiction book about human history that's very relevant to my interests as an educated professional. The price shown is £54.23 and it has a 5-star rating shown on the page.

Let me verify the exact title and price from the browser state:
- Title: "Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind" (from the `title` attribute on element [772])
- Price: £54.23

This is a great choice - it's intellectually stimulating, relevant to someone interested in understanding the world, and popular among professionals. Let me write this to the output file.

results.content:
Data written to file book_interest.json successfully. (auto-corrected from '/app/output/book_interest.json')
Data written to file book_interest.json successfully. (auto-corrected from '/app/output/book_interest.json')

message.text:
Eval: The file book_interest.json was successfully written with the correct title "Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind" and price "£54.23" as seen on the live page. Verdict: Success
Memory: Task complete. Wrote book_interest.json with Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind at £54.23. Both title and price confirmed from live page at books.toscrape.com.
Next goal: Task is complete - call done with results.

steps.reasoning_content: The task is complete. I can see the books.toscrape.com catalog page with "Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind" at £54.23 visible on the page. The file has already been written with the correct title and price from the live page. Let me verify the file contents are correct and call done.

arguments.text:
I browsed books.toscrape.com and selected **Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind** at **£54.23** as my pick. The book was visible on the front page of the catalog with a 5-star rating.

The file `/app/output/book_interest.json` has been written with:
```json
{
  "title": "Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind",
  "price_gbp": "£54.23",
  "interested": true,
  "reason": "As a product manager who thinks a lot about systems and human behavior, Sapiens is exactly the kind of book I'd put on my reading list. Harari's sweep across human history helps me zoom out from daily sprint planning and think about the bigger picture — why we organize the way we do, how collective narratives shape markets and teams. It's the kind of non-fiction that earns its place on a busy professional's shelf."
}
```
Title and price were read directly from the live page.
